frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: RL agents trained on a spatially embedded binary decision task

frameworks.name: multi-region brain model
frameworks.one_line: grid-place-cortex circuit coupled to action-selection RNN

frameworks.name: structured memory circuits
frameworks.one_line: entorhinal-hippocampal architectures as inductive biases for learning

core_question: Which interaction architecture between grid cells, place cells, and cortex best supports efficient learning in spatially embedded decision-making?
key_assumptions: decision-making is spatially embedded and uses evidence accumulation
grid and place cells interact through specifiable circuit architectures
an action-selection cortical recurrent network reads out memory representations
manipulations: interaction architecture between grid and place cells
what signals grid cells receive (self-movement velocity, decision evidence)
counterfactual comparison across alternative circuit architectures
key_findings: grid cells jointly encoding velocity and decision-evidence increments optimize learning efficiency
this architecture best reproduces experimental neural observations relative to alternatives
alternative architectures show reduced learning performance and weaker match to data
interpretations: brain-inspired structured memory architectures provide useful inductive biases for efficient RL
model yields testable predictions about entorhinal-hippocampal-neocortical information flow
